question: What satellite missions are commonly used for monitoring global sea surface temperature, and what spatial resolution do they typically achieve?
structured_context:
## RELATED DOCUMENTS
---
# Title: Sentinel-3 Sea Surface Temperature Monitoring
# Content: The Sentinel-3 mission carries the SLSTR instrument which measures sea surface temperature at 1 km resolution. MODIS on Aqua and Terra also provides SST data at similar resolutions. These missions enable global ocean monitoring for climate research and weather forecasting applications.
---
# Title: VIIRS SST Products from Suomi NPP
# Content: The VIIRS instrument on Suomi NPP satellite provides sea surface temperature products at 750 m resolution. The data is used in operational oceanography and has demonstrated accuracy comparable to in-situ measurements from drifting buoys.

question: How is synthetic aperture radar (SAR) used for flood mapping, and what are the main limitations?
structured_context:
## RELATED DOCUMENTS
---
# Title: SAR-Based Flood Detection Methods
# Content: Synthetic aperture radar is widely used for flood mapping due to its ability to penetrate clouds. Sentinel-1 C-band SAR data is commonly used. Key limitations include double-bounce effects in urban areas and difficulty distinguishing flooded vegetation from open water.
